steps.title: Condition for no solution: parallel lines
steps.content: A system has no solution when the two lines are parallel — they have the same slope but different y-intercepts.

steps.title: Find the choice with slope 6 and a different y-intercept
steps.content:
The given equation $y = 6x + 18$ has slope 6 and y-intercept 18. Rewrite each choice in slope-intercept form:
  • A: $-6x + y = 18$ → $y = 6x + 18$ (same equation)
  • B: $-6x + y = 22$ → $y = 6x + 22$ (slope 6, y-intercept 22)
  • C: $-12x + y = 36$ → $y = 12x + 36$ (slope 12)
  • D: $-12x + y = 18$ → $y = 12x + 18$ (slope 12)
Only choice B has slope 6 (matching the given equation) with a different y-intercept (22 ≠ 18), creating parallel lines with no intersection. That matches choice B — B is correct.
